notes: Didactic fit is strong: the playable mechanic maps 1:1 to the concept (host contract = docking-port connector shape; structural contract check = clamp green/red; sandbox cap = force-field bubble; enforcement = boundary throw). Four levels (L1 dock predict, L2 missing method, L3 allow/block sandbox, L4 minimal capability set) each teach a distinct facet of sandboxing + interface contracts. Sim core is pure WebGL-free TS, exercised identically in Vitest and the browser; invoke() throws SandboxViolation on out-of-cap calls, and capabilities cannot escalate after dock. NOT a thematic reskin. Note: prior .loops/.../evidence.json in the output dir is a stale/alternate-schema record (source:plugindoj, lifecycle metrics) from an earlier pixelDojo design pass — it does NOT match this voxelDojo game; the authoritative EVIDENCE is the console record asserted by the green smoke, whose shape conforms to the voxeldojo schema. decision.md similarly describes a different (5-state lifecycle) design; the built game follows docs/plans/09_plugin_system.md (DOCKING BAY contract+sandbox). All required items hold.
